question: What is the primary problem your project aims to solve?
answer: Improve existing solution - Enhance or replace current UI that's inadequate

question: What is the core value proposition of this UI improvement?
answer: Better user experience - A locked in, repeatable set of steps for walking users through the sherpy workflow

question: What is in scope for this UI refactor?
answer: Complete UI overhaul - Redesign the entire interface and user flow

question: What is explicitly out of scope for this project?
answer: All of the above - No backend/API changes, only sherpy workflow (not other product areas), desktop/web only (no mobile), no new features (only refactoring existing capabilities)

question: Who are the primary users of this refactored UI?
answer: Product/project managers - Non-technical stakeholders planning projects

question: What are the primary goals of your users when using this interface?
answer: Complete project planning efficiently and create documentation - Get through the sherpy workflow quickly and accurately while generating comprehensive planning artifacts

question: What are the main pain points users experience with the current UI?
answer: All of the above - Confusing navigation (hard to know what step comes next), too much flexibility (easy to skip steps or go off track), unclear progress (dont know where they are in the workflow), manual process (too much typing, not guided enough)

question: What is the primary use case for this interface?
answer: All of the above with MAIN POINT being usage of comprehensive sherpy-flow skills in repo - New project intake, continuing existing projects, and reviewing completed plans, all driven by the sherpy-flow skill system

question: What is the typical user journey through the interface?
answer: Guided but flexible - Mostly linear workflow with ability to go back or skip with warnings. Users may stop after initial generation of business requirements and return to add new requirements before continuing to next steps

question: What are the must-have UI components for the new interface?
answer: All of the above - Step indicator/progress sidebar (visual display of workflow steps and current position), tabbed content area (separate views for Chat and Files), auto-submission (skills automatically invoked without manual commands)

question: How should the sidebar workflow display behave?
answer: Fixed position with interactive step navigation - Always visible taking up 1/3 screen width, click steps to navigate, show completion status with icons (✓, →, ○)

question: How should the Chat tab function during the guided workflow?
answer: Hybrid approach - Primarily guided interview but allow occasional free-form messages

question: What should the Files tab display?
answer: All of the above - Generated artifacts (all YAML, MD files created by workflow in organized structure), file tree (hierarchical view of requirements/, implementation/, delivery/, etc.), file preview (click to view file contents inline)

question: How should skill invocation work in the new UI?
answer: Fully automatic - Skills auto-invoked based on workflow step, no user action needed

question: How should the interface handle resuming partially completed projects?
answer: Database-driven state tracking with cascade updates - Database should know workflow status. User can go back to any step to add more information. If earlier steps (like business requirements) are changed after later steps are completed, system needs a way to walk through all succeeding steps to add new info to existing docs based on updated requirements

question: What are your performance expectations for the UI?
answer: All of the above plus streaming AI responses - Instant UI responsiveness (<100ms), fast skill invocations with progress indicators, smooth transitions for step navigation and tab switching, plus streaming AI responses for real-time feedback

question: What are your usability requirements?
answer: All of the above plus keyboard navigation - Intuitive navigation (users understand interface without training), clear visual feedback (always clear what step youre on and whats next), minimal cognitive load (reduce decision fatigue through guided workflow), plus keyboard navigation support

question: What are your accessibility requirements?
answer: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and keyboard navigation - Meet standard accessibility guidelines, all features accessible via keyboard. Use shadcn components for implementation

question: How will you measure the success of this UI refactor?
answer: User satisfaction - Qualitative feedback on ease of use

question: What specific outcomes define success for this project?
answer: All of the above - Users can complete workflow without help (no documentation or support needed), zero confusion about next steps (always clear what to do next), faster project planning (reduced time from start to completed artifacts)

question: What are the technical constraints for this project?
answer: All of the above - Must use existing tech stack (no new frameworks or major dependencies), must work with existing backend/API (no changes to server-side code), must integrate with sherpy-flow skills (core requirement to use skill system)

question: What are the business constraints for this project?
answer: No major constraints - Flexibility on budget, timeline, resources

question: What are the key dependencies for this project?
answer: Existing sherpy-flow skills and Backend API for project/workflow state - Skills must be functional and available, database integration for tracking. All information stored in database with files only created on demand. File visualization in UI done programmatically based on specifications in docs/specifications folder

question: What is your target timeline for this UI refactor?
answer: ASAP - As soon as possible, highest priority and urgency

question: What is the MVP scope for the first release?
answer: Full feature set - Everything including cascade updates when requirements change

question: What are the biggest risks or unknowns for this project?
answer: All of the above - Technical complexity (uncertain how to implement cascade updates or state management), skill integration (unclear how to auto-invoke skills from UI), UX design (need to validate interface actually improves user experience), timeline risk (ASAP timeline with full feature set may be challenging)

question: What assumptions are you making about this project?
answer: Database schema exists - Assumption that backend can store workflow state
